steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is an Inferences question. The blank must logically complete a claim that follows from the study described: behavioral similarities between breeds correspond to genetic similarities. The sentence states that French bulldogs and bullmastiffs show similar trainability, so the blank must draw the inference the study's framework supports.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — French bulldogs and bullmastiffs show a greater tendency toward trainability than most other dog breeds do.: The passage makes no claim about how these breeds rank against all other breeds; this goes beyond what the study supports. ✗
  • Choice B — the two breeds will likely become less genetically similar over time.: This reverses the study's logic — if behavior predicts genetic similarity, similar trainability suggests shared genetics now, not future divergence. ✗
  • Choice C — individual French bulldogs likely display higher levels of trainability than individual bullmastiffs.: This introduces a comparison between individual dogs within breeds, which the study's framework of breed-level genetic similarities does not support. ✗
  • Choice D — their similarities with respect to that behavior could result from a shared aspect of their genetics.: This directly applies the study's finding — behavioral similarity corresponds to genetic similarity — to the French bulldog and bullmastiff case. ✓
That is the correct answer.
